## Slides: Shared short-read logic

1. Add platform-specific adapters
2. Produce detectable clonal copies
3. Measure nucleotide incorporation cycle by cycle
4. Convert measurements into bases and quality scores
5. Export demultiplexed reads

## Slides: Illumina

- Fragments form clonal clusters on a flow cell
- Reversible-terminator sequencing-by-synthesis
- Broad instrument and library ecosystem
- Familiar error profiles and mature pipelines
- Established reference point for compatibility and benchmarking

::: {.notes}
Give Illumina equal teaching time with Ultima. Emphasize both chemistry and the value of the mature ecosystem.
:::

## Slides: Ultima

- Molecules are clonally amplified into beads
- Beads load onto a high-density open fluidics surface
- Flow-based sequencing-by-synthesis
- Architecture optimized for sequencing at large scale
- ppmSeq can use complementary-strand information for accurate SNV detection

::: {.notes}
Keep the description conceptual. Mention deep sequencing, rare variants, and cell-free DNA as relevant applications without turning this into a product catalogue.
:::

## Slides: ppmSeq

![](figures/ultima2.png){width=82%}

![](figures/ultima1.png){width=82%}

## Slides: Illumina and Ultima

| Illumina | Shared | Ultima |
|---|---|---|
| Mature ecosystem | High throughput | Scale-focused architecture |
| Broad compatibility | High per-base accuracy | Open fluidics surface |
| Deeply tested pipelines | Short-read evidence | ppmSeq workflows |

Their biological limitations are more similar to each other than to long-read platforms.

## Slides: What short reads do well

- SNP and small-indel genotyping
- Cohort-scale allele frequencies
- RNA-seq and functional assays
- Metagenomic profiling
- Deep coverage of represented graph paths
- Mapping many samples to a pangenome graph

## Slides: Where short reads struggle

- Repeats longer than the reads
- Structural variants and novel insertions
- Segmental duplications and paralogous loci
- Long-range phasing
- Complex de novo assembly
- Sequences absent from the mapping reference

```text
Reference:    A -------- B
Sample:       A -- [ long insertion ] -- B

Short reads:  A --->       ?       <--- B
Long read:    A ------------------------> B
                    spans the event
```
